pattern: A standard partition DP over prefix sums - best score splitting the first i elements into j subarrays - handles the triangular subarray cost directly, since it depends only on each piece's sum.
hints: The cost of one subarray only depends on its sum, so precompute prefix sums and you can price any subarray in O(1).
This is a partition-into-exactly-k-groups problem: let dp[j][i] be the minimum score splitting the first i elements into j subarrays, built from dp[j-1][m] for every earlier cut point m.
dp[j][i] = min over m < i of dp[j-1][m] + triangular(prefix[i]-prefix[m]), where triangular(s) = s*(s+1)/2; the answer is dp[k][n].

edge_inputs.description: k equals the array length: every element is its own subarray
k is one: the whole array is a single subarray
an isolated spike should end up isolated in its own subarray
all equal elements: partitions should balance evenly
k is between one and the array length

stress.description: moderately large array split into a random number of parts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
